characters.name: Alberticus Lax
characters.description: Alberticus Laxis a wizard who created the "Chalice of So-Whatitude".

characters.name: Annie
characters.description: AnniePhysical DescriptionSpeciesMonsterGenderFemaleAgeUnknownPersonal InformationFamilyJobeaux(husband)ResidenceRedneckistanChronological and Political InformationFirst Appearance"Foreign Exchange Problem"Last Appearance"Division Quest"PortrayerNovie Edwards Annieis the name of a side character from the second season of theYin Yang Yo!series. She is the wife ofJobeauxand lives inRedneckistan. Annie appears as an anthropomorphic gynoid with pale magenta skin, necklong dark magenta hair and two white horns on her head. Her eyelids are dark lavender and sport long, accentuated lashes. She uses bloodred lipstick and wears a bright magenta-pink dress. As a Redneckistan, she wears no shoes but walks barefoot instead. Not much can be said about her true personality, as her entrance as her true self during her debut inForeign Exchange Problemis very short and in the episodeDivision Quest, she was brainwashed byElla Mental. However, it can be said with certainty that she is pretty mature, caring and organized and she seems to love Jobeaux very much.

characters.name: Badfoot
characters.description: BadfootPhysical DescriptionSpeciesHorseGenderMaleAgeUnknownPersonal InformationFamilyUnknownMinionsHis three musiciansEnemiesYinYangMaster YoResidenceBadfoot's lairChronological and Political InformationOccupationsCriminal entertainer and musicianFirst Appearance"The Big Payback"PortrayerOrlando Jones Badfootis the name of a recurring minor villain of theYin Yang Yo!series. He's a criminal musician and entertainer who seeks to humiliateMaster Yoand steal hisWoo Fooat all costs. His appearance and way of speaking are a parody of famous real-life music legend and master of Funk and House, James Brown. Badfoot appears as an anthropomorphic horse. His skin is dark grey-ish with a purple-blue hue, his hooves are black. He sports black ears and a thick, black mane styled in popular 80's Fringe hair style. He has googly eyes with small, ruby red irisses and slightly bulgy lips. He dresses in extravagant, glittery costumes consisting of a pink long-sleeved blouse, a purple catsuit with a scarlet sash and a large purple cape. Badfoot sports a disagreeable, boisterous and mildly obnoxious personality. He is loud-mouthed, boastful, arrogant and self-centered. He is also success-pampered and reckless, valueing nothing more than his ego and fame. He also proves shamelessness when he takes a whole town hostage just to force Master Yo into showing up to a funk duel he knows Yo couldn't win. He even doesn't shy away from taking Yin and Yang hostage in attempt to get his Lick-n-Stick back. Over all, Badfoot truely lives up to his name - in a bad and shameful way.

characters.name: Boogeyman
characters.description: BoogeymanPhysical DescriptionSpeciesMonsterGenderMaleAgeUnknownPersonal InformationFamilyUnknownFriendsYinYangWoo Foo ArmyEnemiesAnyone opposing Yin, Yang and their friendsResidenceUnknownWeaponsVinyl recordsChronological and Political InformationOccupationsDJWoo Foo KnightFirst Appearance"Party Favors"PortrayerOrlando Jones Boogeymanis the name of a recurring character of the second season of theYin Yang Yo!series. He is a disco-loving green monster. Not much is known about Boogeyman, as he's only appeared in a couple of episodes to which he was significant to. Boogeyman appears as an anthropomorphic, slim and lanky monster with pale greenish and scaly skin. He has dark violet, bushy and curly hair styled into an impressive afrolook (although it might be actually a wig). He also sports two long, slim horns on the sides of his head, pointing away in right ankles. His true eye colour is unknown, as he always wears pink-framed, round sunglasses. He dresses in a fancy disco outfit reminiscent of the 1980's. It consists of a dark pink blouse with a large, erect collar and dark pink flare trousers with a purple belt that has a large, round buckle with a blue star on it. He also wears dark violet plateau shoes with high, black shoe soles. Boogeyman sports a funky, lively and sympathic personality, he seems pretty mature for his age. He is friendly and kind, but also attentive and open-minded. He has a great sense of music and he loves to party and lift up festive moods. He is also a talented DJ and stylist, he can even show off some cool moves on the dance floor. Boogeyman speaks with a severe slang reminiscent of late 80's Funk style. This sometimes serves as a running gag throughout the series, although it actually makes it hard to understand him.
characters.personality_section: Boogeyman sports a funky, lively and sympathic personality, he seems pretty mature for his age. He is friendly and kind, but also attentive and open-minded. characters.name: Boopy Von Ha-Ha Pants
characters.description: Boopy Von Ha-Ha PantsPhysical DescriptionSpeciesClownGenderMaleAgeUnknownPersonal InformationFamilyUnknownResidenceCircus tentWeaponsAnything a clown uses for pranksChronological and Political InformationOccupationsClown-FumasterProfessional Clown and JesterFirst Appearance"Gone-A-Fowl"Last Appearance"Clown-Fu Fighting"PortrayerJonathan Wilson Boopy Von Ha-Ha Pantsis a recurring minor character of theYin Yang Yo!series. He is the master ofClown-Fuand fosters a strong rivalry withMaster Yo. Despite this, he is not considered a real antagonist or villain, as he never attacksYin,Yangor any bystanders. Boopy is an anthropomorphic humanoid with vanilla coloured skin. He has long, orange hair twisted into step curls on each side. He also sports a very long handlebar mustache that stretches horizontally left and right. His eyes are magenta in colour and he wears a red honk nose. His lips are generously glossed in red. Boopy dresses in a classical clown attire, consisting of a blue tricorn hat with a long, pink feather boa; a blue pant with suspensers; a white shirt with a red, yellow-dotted bowtie; red boxing gloves and riddiculously over-sized, pink shoes. Not much can be said about his personality, as he appears only twice and only to challange Master Yo into a clown duel. His appearances, however, reveal that he is very talented and takes his clown profession very serious. He is also revealed to be an enthusiastic collector of bowties and suspenders.
characters.personality_section: Not much can be said about his personality, as he appears only twice and only to challange Master Yo into a clown duel. His appearances, however, reveal that he is very talented and takes his clown profession very serious. He is also revealed to be an enthusiastic collector of bowties and suspenders.

characters.name: Brett
characters.description: Brett is a one-time character onYin Yang Yo!. He appears in the episode “Falling Yin Love”, and serves as Yin’s crush, but is actually a robot worn byYuck, who is a villain throughout the series. Basically he is everything Yin is attracted to, such as his eyes, hairstyle, and knee-caps.

characters.name: Carl the Evil Cockroach Wizard
characters.description: Carl the Evil Cockroach WizardPhysical DescriptionSpeciesCockroachGenderMaleAgeUnknownPersonal InformationStatusAliveFamilyEdna(mother)Herman(brother)FriendsnoneMinionsNoneResidenceCarl's Evil LairWeaponsHis inventionsChronological and Political InformationOccupationsSorcererSupervillainLeader and Founder Member of the League of EvilFirst Appearance"Finding Hershel"Last Appearance"Yin Yang Who?"PortrayerJamie Watson Carl the Evil Cockroach Wizard, or simplyCarl, is one of the two main villains of theYin Yang Yo!series. He is an insectoid criminal who seeks world domination. Carl suffers from a severe case of Oedipus-complex, as he non-top tries to win his mother'sEdnalove and affection and out-do his brotherHerman, despite being actually much stronger than him. Because of that (and because he is clumsy and overly self-centered) he is doomed to fail in pretty much every endeavor he seeks to attain. Carl appears as an anthropomorphic red cockroach with oddly bent antennae, thick black eyebrows and green eyes. His mouth sports three protruding, white and pointy teeth visible even when his mouth is shut. He has no visible nose or ears. He sports two arms with hands and four legs with roundish feet. Carl wears a dark lilac cape with a white star pattern on it and a dark lilac shoulder belt with a skull-shaped beltknuckle. Carl, the Evil Cockroach Wizard (who tends to recite his name as often and loud as possible), continuously schemes to defeat Yin, Yang, Master Yo and the power of the Woo Foo with his insecure genius. He also suffers from an Oedipus-complex: he still lives with his overbearing mother Edna, who is a dragon. Carl is always trying to find a way to impress his mother and out-do his brother Herman, as he believes that he is the only one who deserves being loved by her. It was shown in the episode "Mission Yinpossible" that Carl's heart's desire was for his mother to love him more than Herman (even though he himself believed that it was to rule the world), but after realizing how she was suffocating him with love, he no longer yearns for it. A bit of a priss, it was shown in "Get Off My Back" that he is an excellent cook and a great groomer. Carl is all-time severely jealous towards his brother Herman, who, unfortunately, is indeed favoured by Edna due to his success in raiding and plundering. Additionally, Heman owns a huge army of ants and Carl is a clumsy loner. This leads to an ever-lasting rivalry between the brothers with Herman almost always having the upper hand. However, despite the differences between Carl and Herman, they have worked together on occasion, making a deadly force against Yin and Yang. Carl seems to have a thing for blocking out the Sun as shown is Episodes likeAn Inconvenient Tooth,Too Much YangformationandYin Yang You(which is accurate for a cockroach, as most cockroaches hate the sunlight). His idea was stolen fromHermanand theNight Masterhimself, which still irritates him to this day. In "Cat Smash Fever", Carl

characters.name: CHAD
characters.description: CHAD talks CHADis the digital motherboard that makes its only appearance in the episode,Moon Over My Yinnie. This is the computer ofYin, theWoo-Foo Battleshiprepaired by her to a boyfriend, but it was destroyed in the only episode it appeared in by the MOTHERSHIP, made byCarl. It was voiced by Kyle Massey. It hates people who make fun ofYinmuch likeYang Quotes: "You laugh at Yin you laugh at me punk!" "This place sucks yo lets bounce" - CHAD,Moon Over My Yinnie

characters.name: Chai and Ti
characters.description: Tai & ChiPhysical DescriptionSpeciesTiger (both)GenderMale (Chai)Female (Ti)AgeUnknownPersonal InformationFamilyMaster Yo(adoptive son & student)Yin(adoptive granddaughter)Yang(adoptive grandson)Chronological and Political InformationOccupationsWoo Foo WarriorsFirst Appearance"Doomed to repeat it"Last Appearance"Yin Yang Who?" Chai and Tiare two recurring minor side characters of theYin Yang Yo!series. They served asMaster Yo's adoptive parents and teachers until they were killed byEradicus. Their names are a pun onTai Chi, the Chinese principle of opposites attract. Tai and Chi are both anthropomorphic tigers.Chaiis the male, with orange fur sporting dark brown stripes. His chest, belly, lower face and undersite of his arms are of a very pale beige, almost white. He has emerald green eyes with black cat pupils. He sports bushy, dark brown eyebrows and a small, dark brown goatie on his chin. His cheek hairs flare away from the cheeks and his huge upper fangs always protrude from his mouth. His chest is huge and his belly has a six pack. Chai wears a red bandana, red wrist bands and red pants.Tiis the female, with skyblue fur sporting dark blue stripes. Her lower face is pale blue, which is also the case for her belly and the undersites of her arms. She has big, almond-shaped, white eyes with round, black pupils. Ti wears a red dress and red wrist bands. Not much can be told about their personalities, as their appearances are very short. However, in two flashbacks it is shown that they argued a lot during their youth. Most of the time, their arguments were the result of Chai being boisterous and reckless, while Ti was more of a tactical thinker and rather tame. They also often disagreed on which Woo Foo power was the better. Despite their opposite personalities, they seem to have loved each other very much.

characters.name: Charles
characters.description: CharlesCharlesBiographical InformationReal Full NameCharlesOther NamesServant CharlesPhysical DescriptionSpeciesAntGenderMaleAgeUnknownPersonal InformationStatusAliveFamilyUnknownFriendsG.P.MinionsAnt ArmyEnemiesYinYangMaster YoEquipmentsBlack Steel Armoured SuitResidenceHerman’s CastleWeaponsSwordChronological and Political InformationOccupationsBrother Herman's Second-in-CommandAffiliationTBAFirst AppearanceDictator of the YearLast AppearanceSplitting HaresPortrayerTBA Charles isBrother Herman's second in command it is for this reason that he usually finds himself in a leadership roles during operations. While Herman doesn't particularly care about any of his soldiers, he has placed some degree trust in Charles, as he is often tasked to collect objects that Herman specifically requires. Charles is extremely obedient, though he has questioned his loyalty on certain occasions. Charles is fond of long walks along the beach and loves sugar, even going so far to procastinates his duty for the latter. Charles is fond of long walks along the beach and loves sugar, even going so far to procastinates his duty for the latter.

characters.name: Clamuel the Clamboy
characters.description: Clamuel the Clamboyis really a clam boy and has an appearance inFor the Love of Clamboy.Yinfound him under the sea and saw his jewels. Clamboy asked Yin if she could be his friend. Yin accepted but wanted nothing more than his jewels. Later,Yangfound an interesting information: Yin must be married with Clamuel 100 clam years. If she won't he turns into a big monster but Yin can't hear the information. The problem was that Clamuel must have a kiss today. Otherwise, the same thing would happen. Afterwards, Yin doesn't accepted to marry and kiss Clamuel. He was sad and turns into the evil monster. Yin and Yang could defeat him and Yin accepted to marry him for 100 clam years. Clamuel turns now into a very strong and handsome man. Yin fell in love but 100 clam years are for other people (or animals) only 30 minutes. The end of the story was that Yin has no boyfriend anymore and no more jewels.

characters.name: Coop
characters.description: CoopPhysical DescriptionSpeciesChickenGenderMaleAgeUnknownPersonal InformationFamilyCoop's Mother(mother)ResidenceCoop's homeWeaponsNoneChronological and Political InformationOccupationsServant of theNight Master(formerly)Woo Foo Knight in a Little Less Training (currently)First Appearance"Finding Hershel"Last Appearance"Yin Yang Who?"PortrayerJohnathan Wilson Coopis the name of a recurring side character from the Yin Yang Yo! series. He's a skittish and cranky nerd who appears to be scared of any threats and has a huge crush onYin. At first, she is embarassed and tries to avoid him at all costs. Later, after discovering his dark side, she eventually returns his attraction. Of all characters of the series, Coop undergoes the most vast and complex character change and -development. Coop appears as an anthropomorphic young rooster with a bright yellow plummage and a bright orange beak. His dark orange top hair sports a side parting and he has freckles. He has rather small googly eyes with blue irisses. His body stature is thin and meakly, but can turn into an athletic muscleman when he becomes emotionally unstable or is ticked off. However, he loses this peculiarity at the end of the first season. Coop's dresscode represents the stereotypical nerd: he wears round glasses, dental braces, blue jeans, a white poloshirt and black, shiny low shoes. He also wears sweatbands at his wrists. Coop stutters, especially when nervous. Coop sports a whimsical, scittish and rather cowardly personality. He flees dangerous situations at first sight and shys away from vocal or physical confrontations. However, he does not shy away to openly display his nerdish personality, bravely ignoring the derision and malice he receives from bystanders (including Yin andYang). He also ignores the fact that his advances towards Yin embarass her greatly. He also tends to berate others whenever they seem to be up to no good, making him the voice of reasons occasonally. Otherwise, he is always honest and open towards peoples he gets along with rather well (likeDave, for example). It needs a lot of emotional pressure and provocation to awaken his hidden dark side. Since he has a huge crush on Yin (very much to her dismay) and she rejects him constantly, Coop soon turns out to be a ticking time bomb. This character weakness is soon discovered byThe Nightmaster, who turns Coop into his spy in exchange for the promise to get Yin as his girlfriend. Unfortunately, Coop falls for it, as he sees no other option to get his desire fulfilled. Coop also likes corn a lot, which he refers to as "Nummy yummy Corn" and eats it in the same manner as a real chicken.

characters.name: Coop's Mother
characters.description: Appearance: Coop's mom wears a pinkish - purple shirt, skirt and high heels. Description:Coop's Mother is a publicist and very pretty. Most of the kids who attended her 'Thanks for saving the world all by Yourself by Banishing the Night Master' party, only went there to see her.  Her husband has never been seen. She was one of the 'chicks' who loved Master Yo's old band 'Pandangerous'. In the episode, "The Secret Life of Possum Panda", it is shown that she lives a double life as a villainess named, "Hot Chick". She is voiced by American actress Jennifer Coolidge.

characters.name: Dank and Dire
characters.description: Dank and DireDank (left) and Dire (right)Physical DescriptionSpeciesDemonsGenderMale (both)AgeUnknownPersonal InformationFriendsNoneEnemiesYinYangMaster YoResidenceNight Master's LairWeaponsNoneChronological and Political InformationOccupationsNight Master's minionsFirst AppearanceDestination DangerLast AppearanceCreeping With the EnemyPortrayerDwayne Hill(Dank)Martin Roach(Dire) Dank and Direare the two minions of theNight Masterin Season 1 of theYin Yang Yo!series. Dank and Direare two dark creatures who seem to be made of dark energy or shadow. They might be actual shadow figures animated by the Night Master's umbracinetic powers. Both are anthropomorphic, completely black and only their eyes and mouthes are visible (because they are white). Both, however, are distinguishable due their body shapes:Dankis rather short, almost quadratic in shape and sports two horns on his head. His hands and feet have claws and resemble that of birds.Dire, instead, is slender, rectangular and sports three cone-shaped appendages on his head. His hands are two (sometimes three) roundish stubs and his feet resemble hoofs.Bothhave short tails with pointy, triangular ends. Even if they're villains, they're shown to be generally calm and don't like being yelled at, as shown inDestination Danger. They also are shown to be obidient, diligent and creative at one side - but they are likewise infantile and incompetent. It's worringly easy to distract them or rile them up against each other, thus foiling their plans quickly. However, they shouldn't be underastimated, especially when they work together in harmony.

characters.name: Dave
characters.description: DavePhysical DescriptionSpeciesTree peoplesGenderMaleAgeUnknownPersonal InformationFamilyDave's Father(father)Dave's Mother(mother)Chip(brother)ResidenceForestWeaponsHis leavesChronological and Political InformationOccupationsWoo Foo Knight in a Little Less TrainingFirst Appearance"Finding Hershel"Last Appearance"Girl Bunnies"PortrayerJohnathan Wilson Dave the Enchanted Tree Stump, or simply justDave, is the name of a recurring side character from theYin Yang Yo!series. He's a cranky nerd and skittish scaredy-cat who undergoes a tremendous character change and -developement throughout the series. During first season, he discovers his secret ability to communicate with trees and to use his own body as weaponry and protection. Dave appears as an somewhat anthropomorphic tree stump with a hazelnut coloured, smooth bark. His whole body is cubic in shape and he sports short, tubby legs. He has slim arms and has green leaves as eyebrows. A short branch stump protrudes from his forehead, wearing three green leaves. His round eyes sport purple irisses and he has freckles. His nose ist short and square. Dave wears a red cap on his head pointing backwards. He also owns a dark orange-brown coloured school backpack. Dave is a highly insecure boy, he often acts whiny and girlish while also sporting lots of naivety and curiosity. With his overall persona he often becomes the target of ridicule and malice, especially fromYangandRoger Jr.. Despite this, he never gives up to befriend new characters and try to be the voice of reason. During first season, he discovers that he has a hidden power he never new about: he can telepathically communcate with trees and ask them for advise and even battle support. This discovery builds the key event to his future character development. It gives him courage and self-esteem, slowly turning Dave from a whiny scaredy-cat into a brave teenager. Dave learns to stand up for himself and confront those who are mean to him. The fact that, as a living tree stump, he cannot be really harmed by fists and/or weapons, also strengthens his confidence. His favorite hobbies include various board games includingWowza-WowzaandGiggity-Giggity.

characters.description: Dave's FatherPhysical DescriptionSpeciesMagical talking tree stumpGenderMalePersonal InformationFamilyDave's Mother(wife)Dave(son)Chip(son)ResidenceForest Dave's parents
